purpose: Sourced candidate list for scattering-native comparator work under PG1-PG8 physical-regime source-intake criteria. This queues theory-native S-matrix/positive-geometry sources and physical scattering-matrix observable sources for later source review, without declaring scattering-native physics a winner or substrate.

critical_review_prework.hard_bottleneck_attacked: scattering-native comparator uncertainty: whether S-matrix/positive-geometry structure is only a mathematical comparator or can connect to physical observables, intrinsic readout, harnessability, and bulk/readout non-faithfulness
thin_stick_risks: positive-geometry theorem treated as physical substrate evidence
S-matrix analyticity/unitarity constraints treated as native Formation Medium settling law
scattering-matrix optical observables treated as canonical positive-geometric readout
mathematical proximity to amplitudes treated as winner status
critical_review_prework.negative_progress_risk: Could become a list of beautiful amplitude papers plus unrelated wave-scattering devices unless every class is scored against PG gates and kill notes.
critical_review_prework.why_not_convenient_drift: It forces the scattering-native comparator to face physical-observable and harnessability gates instead of winning by mathematical proximity.

anti_flattening_rule: Scattering-native is a mandatory law-native comparator. It is not CRBSM, not Route A, not high-resonance bridge status, not selected substrate, and not the default winner by mathematical proximity.

candidate_classes.label: positive geometry / canonical-form amplitude structures
candidate_classes.why_candidate: This class is the closest mathematical comparator to Formation Medium readout language: canonical forms, boundary recursion, scattering forms, and amplitude-positive geometry relations.
kill_notes: canonical form is not physical readout by itself
positive geometry is not substrate evidence
auxiliary-space geometry may fail physical harnessability
source-backed amplitude structure must not become Formation Medium theorem status

candidate_classes.label: S-matrix bootstrap / analyticity-unitarity-positivity constraints
candidate_classes.why_candidate: This class tests whether native law constraints on scattering amplitudes provide admissibility and observable bounds closer to Formation Medium gates than engineered mechanisms.
kill_notes: bounds on amplitudes are not native settling dynamics
analyticity/unitarity/crossing constraints are not hardware carrier variables
EFT admissibility is not Formation Medium admissibility
bootstrap observables may remain abstract and non-harnessable

candidate_classes.label: physical scattering-matrix topology and phase singularity observables
candidate_classes.why_candidate: This class connects scattering-matrix eigenvalue topology, degeneracies, phase vortices, and experimental observables in open photonic/metasurface systems.
kill_notes: scattering phase singularities are not canonical positive-geometric readout
open photonic observables may be measurement artifacts
topological scattering features may remain device engineering
requires negative controls against Route A and high-resonance hardware/proxy roles

candidate_classes.label: scattering zeros / coherent perfect absorption / exceptional-point observables
candidate_classes.why_candidate: This class tests whether zeros of scattering operators, coherent perfect absorption, and exceptional structures provide intrinsic readout-like observables in wave systems.
kill_notes: perfect absorption is not Formation Medium settling
S-matrix zeros are not automatically canonical readout
wavefront shaping or coherent input control can make the effect externally programmed
exceptional-point behavior can be high-resonance hardware, not scattering-native substrate

source_candidates.title: Positive geometries and canonical forms
source_candidates.track_relevance: canonical forms and positive geometry relation to scattering amplitudes
source_candidates.candidate_value: strongest readout-semantics comparator for PG5 and PG6 at mathematical level
source_candidates.blocked_inference: positive geometry canonical forms are not physical substrate or intrinsic apparatus readout

source_candidates.title: Scattering forms and the positive geometry of kinematics, color and the worldsheet
source_candidates.track_relevance: scattering forms and positive geometry directly in kinematic space
source_candidates.candidate_value: tests whether scattering-native structure is less auxiliary and closer to intrinsic readout
source_candidates.blocked_inference: kinematic-space positive geometry is not Formation Medium substrate selection

source_candidates.title: Nonperturbative negative geometries: amplitudes at strong coupling and the amplituhedron
source_candidates.track_relevance: geometry-connected IR-finite observable and strong-coupling amplitude structure
source_candidates.candidate_value: candidate bridge from canonical/negative geometry to observables under PG5
source_candidates.blocked_inference: IR-finite observable relation is not physical Formation Medium readout

source_candidates.title: Momentum Amplituhedron for N=6 Chern-Simons-Matter Theory: Scattering Amplitudes from Configurations of Points in Minkowski Space

source_candidates.candidate_value: tests whether geometry/readout proximity improves outside standard planar N=4 SYM setting
source_candidates.blocked_inference: momentum-amplituhedron formulation is not physical substrate evidence

source_candidates.title: Rigorous Bounds on the Analytic S Matrix
source_candidates.track_relevance: rigorous analyticity/crossing/unitarity bounds in S-matrix bootstrap
source_candidates.candidate_value: strong PG2 admissibility comparator for native law constraints
source_candidates.blocked_inference: S-matrix bootstrap bounds are not native settling law or substrate selection

source_candidates.track_relevance: compares positivity and S-matrix bootstrap constraints on EFT amplitudes
source_candidates.candidate_value: helps compare positivity-derived admissibility with stronger bootstrap constraints
source_candidates.blocked_inference: EFT cutoff/bootstrap bounds are not Formation Medium physical regime validation

source_candidates.candidate_value: tests whether more physical scattering channels change admissibility and observables
source_candidates.blocked_inference: inelasticity constraints are not hardware harnessability

source_candidates.title: Positivity properties of scattering amplitudes
source_candidates.track_relevance: recent amplitude positivity properties relevant to law-native admissibility
source_candidates.blocked_inference: positivity properties are not Formation Medium theorem or physical substrate evidence

source_candidates.track_relevance: scattering-matrix eigenvalue degeneracies linked to observable cross-polarization phase vortex lines

source_candidates.track_relevance: perfect absorption in complex scattering systems and hidden-symmetry structure
source_candidates.candidate_value: tests S-matrix zero/readout-like observables in complex wave systems
source_candidates.blocked_inference: perfect absorption is not native settling law

source_candidates.title: Non-linear coherent perfect absorption in the proximity of exceptional points
source_candidates.track_relevance: nonlinear CPA near exceptional-point degeneracies of scattering-function zeros
source_candidates.candidate_value: tests if scattering zeros/exceptional points offer robust observable structure
source_candidates.blocked_inference: nonlinear CPA is not Formation Medium settling or substrate evidence

source_candidates.title: Coherent perfect absorbers: linear control of light with light
source_candidates.track_relevance: review of CPA and scattering-zero control across photonic/material systems
source_candidates.candidate_value: useful negative-control baseline for distinguishing intrinsic readout from coherent input control
source_candidates.blocked_inference: coherent input control is not intrinsic Formation Medium readout

next_action_policy.preferred_next: choose one narrow source review: SN-C3 if targeting physical scattering-matrix observables, SN-C2 if targeting law-native admissibility, or SN-C1 if tightening canonical readout comparison
do_not_do_next: treat positive geometry source support as physical realization
move sources into SOURCE_EXTRACTED_CLAIMS without ingestion and extraction
treat S-matrix bootstrap bounds as native settling law
treat optical scattering-matrix singularities as canonical Formation Medium readout

non_promotion_rule: This PRI-002 artifact is a sourced candidate list only. It does not ingest sources into the repo corpus, extract source claims, validate scattering-native regimes, beat high-resonance comparison, validate Route A, validate CRBSM, select a substrate, prove a settling law, or establish engineering readiness.
